texto: Fear finds its prey in adolescents for the exact same reason fearlessness does. Every cut, scrape, broken arm or cancer is a cut, scrape, broken arm or cancer that has yet to arrive, that is on the sidelines waiting, along with fate and the story of your life, of everything bad that can happen, some of which will happen—but right now, they’re purely theoretical. It is up to the adolescent’s imagination to make them bleed, to make them hurt.
explicacao: Citação de John M. Keller sobre enfrentar incertezas com ação. “Fear finds its prey in adolescents for the exact same reason fearlessness does. Every cut, scrape…” articula reflexao, adolescence, fear em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como fear e fearlessness.

texto: But now books and men had gone their separate ways. Who has the patience for a book? Only a book.
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de John M. Keller que provoca temas ligados a reflexao. Ao questionar “But now books and men had gone their separate ways. Who has the patience for a book? Only a book.”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre reflexao, bibliophiles, books, com foco em books e literature.
